name: Frontend Engineer Pack
description: A starter pack for coding agents building polished, accessible frontend applications with strong TypeScript foundations.

codex.notes: Skills, browser-verification plugin metadata, and portable managed frontend prompts/templates/tools validate and install through dry-run planning for Codex and local coding-agent workflows.

claude-code.notes: Agent Skills, Claude plugin wrapper, and portable managed capabilities are supported for Claude Code users.

recommendation.reason: Best first role pack for web UI and TypeScript frontend work.

useCases: Plan and implement polished frontend user flows with clear components, state models, and responsive behavior.
Run pre-merge quality gates across loading, empty, error, success, permission, mobile, and keyboard states.
Review UI changes for accessibility, design-system fit, visual regression risk, and performance budget impact.
Create browser verification and screenshot plans for production frontend releases.
examplePrompts: Create a UI implementation plan for this feature, including component boundaries, state handling, responsive behavior, accessibility, and tests.
Run a frontend quality gate on this PR and call out missing states, accessibility blockers, visual regressions, and performance risks.
Review this UI against our design system and recommend where to reuse components, tokens, spacing, and interaction patterns.
Build a visual regression plan with viewport matrix, fixtures, interaction states, and release-blocking screenshot diffs.

capabilities.name: Frontend engineer pack guidance
capabilities.content: Use the Frontend Engineer Pack to ship polished, accessible, responsive, and tested UI changes. Ask for the target user flow, design constraints, data states, interaction states, accessibility requirements, browser/device matrix, performance budget, and verification evidence. Always account for loading, empty, error, success, disabled, permission, mobile, keyboard, and reduced-motion states.

capabilities.name: Frontend quality gate
capabilities.content: Review this frontend change before merge. Check loading, error, empty, success, permission, disabled, optimistic, offline, mobile, keyboard, screen reader, reduced-motion, and high-contrast states. Identify missing tests, visual regressions, performance risk, and design-system drift. End with merge readiness and owner/date follow-ups.

capabilities.name: Design system fit review
capabilities.content: Review this UI against the product design system. Check component reuse, spacing, typography, tokens, color semantics, interaction patterns, density, responsive behavior, and whether custom styling is justified. Recommend simpler system-aligned alternatives where appropriate.

capabilities.name: Accessibility before merge
capabilities.content: Run an accessibility before-merge review. Check semantic structure, labels, focus order, keyboard operation, visible focus, contrast, error messaging, dynamic announcements, target sizes, reduced motion, and screen reader naming. Prioritize blockers, then fixes that can follow shortly after launch.

capabilities.name: Frontend performance budget review
capabilities.content: Review this frontend change against a performance budget. Check LCP, INP, CLS, bundle size, hydration cost, image strategy, network waterfalls, client/server boundaries, caching, and third-party scripts. Identify the highest-leverage fixes and what must be measured before release.
